name: HSC aging review supports mechanism breadth and translation boundaries
summary: A 2026 HSC aging review synthesizes intrinsic and extrinsic mechanisms and proposed rejuvenation strategies, but it remains review-level context rather than primary human efficacy evidence.
statement: A 2026 Genome Biology review describes aged HSC phenotypes including reduced self-renewal, lower long-term reconstitution capacity, myeloid-biased differentiation, and clonal hematopoiesis, while treating rejuvenation strategies as emerging concepts rather than validated treated-human aging interventions.
population_or_model: Review-level synthesis of hematopoietic stem-cell aging literature
caveats: Review context is not new primary efficacy evidence.
The source supports mechanism breadth and translation boundaries, not a stage upgrade.
Primary human outcome records remain necessary before changing the public outlook.
